title: SLIP decode error-recovery diverges; Python/Rust RX buffers unbounded (C bounded at 1280)
description: Encode is in-parity (RFC 1055 END/ESC/ESC_END/ESC_ESC identical in all three). Decode diverges on identical noisy input: ESC+invalid byte - Python StreamDecoder silently DROPS the byte (codec.py:97-117), C slip_decode_byte APPENDS raw byte + error stat (slip_transport.c:212-279), Rust appends raw byte (slip.rs:58-66). ESC+END(0xC0): Python drops END, frames MERGE; C appends 0xC0 as payload, frames merge (corruption); Rust TERMINATES the frame (best resync). Max frame: Python UNBOUNDED bytearray, Rust unbounded Vec (slip.rs:301-303) - memory-DoS on a noisy/malicious serial line; C bounded 1280 (SLIP_LCI_MTU) with overflow discard + stats. Also Python is internally inconsistent: strict one-shot decode() raises on bad escape while its StreamDecoder drops. RFC 1055 is silent on error recovery, so this is pure cross-impl divergence. Fix: standardize on the Rust behavior (terminate on ESC+END, drop+stat invalid escapes) across all three; bound Py/Rust buffers at 1280; add SLIP golden vectors (mmgu.18.6).
